body:
**Suggestion:** The virtual-prefix allowlist includes `'/home/'` and `'home/'`, which makes real filesystem paths like `/home/alice/...` automatically treated as "inside workspace." That bypasses boundary confirmation for destructive operations outside the configured project root. Restrict this allowlist to only truly virtual namespaces (for example `'/project/'` and `'/workspace/'`) so real OS paths are still validated. [security]
Severity Level: Critical 🚨 ```mdx - ❌ Destructive CLI ops can modify files outside project root. - ❌ Web HITL boundary checks can miss home-directory escapes. - ⚠️ Desktop workspace validation may silently allow dangerous deletions. ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Observe the virtual-prefix allowlist in `packages/shared/lib/workspace-boundary.ts:54-61`, where `VFS_VIRTUAL_PREFIXES` includes real-looking prefixes `'/home/'` and `'home/'` alongside virtual ones like `'/project/'`. 2. Note that `isOutsideWorkspace()` at `packages/shared/lib/workspace-boundary.ts:162-189` normalizes the target path and then treats **any** path whose normalized form starts with a listed prefix as automatically inside the workspace (`prefixMatch` check at lines 167-171). 3. Configure a workspace root under a project directory (for example via `WORKSPACE_ROOT=/home/alice/projects/my-app`, using `resolveWorkspaceRoot()` at lines 74-88), then call `requiresWorkspaceBoundaryConfirmation('delete', '/home/alice/.ssh/id_rsa')` from any consumer via the re-exports in `packages/shared/cli/lib/workspace-boundary-cli.ts:11-22` or `web/lib/agent-bins/workspace-boundary.ts:19-29`. 4. During that call, `isOutsideWorkspace()` sees `normalizedTarget` as `/home/alice/.ssh/id_rsa`, matches the `'/home/'` prefix in `VFS_VIRTUAL_PREFIXES`, returns `false` at line 171 (meaning "inside workspace"), and `requiresWorkspaceBoundaryConfirmation()` then returns `{ needsConfirmation: false, workspaceRoot: ... }` at lines 211-213, so no boundary confirmation is triggered for deleting `/home/alice/.ssh/id_rsa` even though it lies outside the intended project root. ```
[Fix in Cursor](https://app.codeant.ai/fix-in-ide?tool=cursor&prompt=This%20is%20a%20comment%20left%20during%20a%20code%20review.%0A%0A%2A%2APath%3A%2A%2A%20packages%2Fshared%2Flib%2Fworkspace-boundary.ts%0A%2A%2ALine%3A%2A%2A%2054%3A61%0A%2A%2AComment%3A%2A%2A%0A%09%2ASecurity%3A%20The%20virtual-prefix%20allowlist%20includes%20%60%27%2Fhome%2F%27%60%20and%20%60%27home%2F%27%60%2C%20which%20makes%20real%20filesystem%20paths%20like%20%60%2Fhome%2Falice%2F...%60%20automatically%20treated%20as%20%22inside%20workspace.%22%20That%20bypasses%20boundary%20confirmation%20for%20destructive%20operations%20outside%20the%20configured%20project%20root.%20Restrict%20this%20allowlist%20to%20only%20truly%20virtual%20namespaces%20%28for%20example%20%60%27%2Fproject%2F%27%60%20and%20%60%27%2Fworkspace%2F%27%60%29%20so%20real%20OS%20paths%20are%20still%20validated.%0A%0AValidate%20the%20correctness%20of%20the%20flagged%20issue.%20If%20correct%2C%20How%20can%20I%20resolve%20this%3F%20If%20you%20propose%20a%20fix%2C%20implement%20it%20and%20please%20make%20it%20concise.%0AOnce%20fix%20is%20implemented%2C%20also%20check%20other%20comments%20on%20the%20same%20PR%2C%20and%20ask%20user%20if%20the%20user%20wants%20to%20fix%20the%20rest%20of%20the%20comments%20as%20well.%20if%20said%20yes%2C%20then%20fetch%20all%20the%20comments%20validate%20the%20correctness%20and%20implement%20a%20minimal%20fix%0A) | [Fix in VSCode Claude](https://app.codeant.ai/fix-in-ide?tool=vscode-claude&prompt=This%20is%20a%20comment%20left%20during%20a%20code%20review.%0A%0A%2A%2APath%3A%2A%2A%20packages%2Fshared%2Flib%2Fworkspace-boundary.ts%0A%2A%2ALine%3A%2A%2A%2054%3A61%0A%2A%2AComment%3A%2A%2A%0A%09%2ASecurity%3A%20The%20virtual-prefix%20allowlist%20includes%20%60%27%2Fhome%2F%27%60%20and%20%60%27home%2F%27%60%2C%20which%20makes%20real%20filesystem%20paths%20like%20%60%2Fhome%2Falice%2F...%60%20automatically%20treated%20as%20%22inside%20workspace.%22%20That%20bypasses%20boundary%20confirmation%20for%20destructive%20operations%20outside%20the%20configured%20project%20root.%20Restrict%20this%20allowlist%20to%20only%20truly%20virtual%20namespaces%20%28for%20example%20%60%27%2Fproject%2F%27%60%20and%20%60%27%2Fworkspace%2F%27%60%29%20so%20real%20OS%20paths%20are%20still%20validated.%0A%0AValidate%20the%20correctness%20of%20the%20flagged%20issue.%20If%20correct%2C%20How%20can%20I%20resolve%20this%3F%20If%20you%20propose%20a%20fix%2C%20implement%20it%20and%20please%20make%20it%20concise.%0AOnce%20fix%20is%20implemented%2C%20also%20check%20other%20comments%20on%20the%20same%20PR%2C%20and%20ask%20user%20if%20the%20user%20wants%20to%20fix%20the%20rest%20of%20the%20comments%20as%20well.%20if%20said%20yes%2C%20then%20fetch%20all%20the%20comments%20validate%20the%20correctness%20and%20implement%20a%20minimal%20fix%0A) *(Use Cmd/Ctrl + Click for best experience)*
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** packages/shared/lib/workspace-boundary.ts **Line:** 54:61 **Comment:** *Security: The virtual-prefix allowlist includes `'/home/'` and `'home/'`, which makes real filesystem paths like `/home/alice/...` automatically treated as "inside workspace." That bypasses boundary confirmation for destructive operations outside the configured project root. Restrict this allowlist to only truly virtual namespaces (for example `'/project/'` and `'/workspace/'`) so real OS paths are still validated. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. Once fix is implemented, also check other comments on the same PR, and ask user if the user wants to fix the rest of the comments as well. if said yes, then fetch all the comments validate the correctness and implement a minimal fix ```
👍 | 👎

**Suggestion:** The boundary check compares raw normalized strings and never resolves relative targets against the workspace root, even though the function accepts relative paths. A relative path like `src/file.ts` will not start with an absolute workspace root, so it is incorrectly marked as outside. Resolve relative targets against the workspace root (or cwd) before comparison. [logic error]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ⚠️ In-workspace relative paths always flagged as outside workspace. - ⚠️ CLI TUI may over-prompt for safe file operations. - ⚠️ Web filesystem UI may show unnecessary confirmation dialogs. ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Review `isOutsideWorkspace()` in `packages/shared/lib/workspace-boundary.ts:162-189`, which accepts `targetPath: string` documented as "absolute or relative" (lines 152-160) and immediately normalizes it with `normalizePath(targetPath)` at line 167. 2. `resolveWorkspaceRoot()` (lines 74-88) returns an absolute root (e.g. `WORKSPACE_ROOT=/tmp/workspace`), and `normalizedRoot = normalizePath(root)` at line 176 yields a normalized absolute path such as `/tmp/workspace`. 3. Call `requiresWorkspaceBoundaryConfirmation('delete', 'test.txt')` (relative path) from any consumer via `packages/shared/cli/lib/workspace-boundary-cli.ts:11-22` or `web/lib/agent-bins/workspace-boundary.ts:19-29`; inside that function (`packages/shared/lib/workspace-boundary.ts:201-223`), it delegates to `isOutsideWorkspace(targetPath, workspaceRoot)` at line 211. 4. In `isOutsideWorkspace()`, `normalizePath('test.txt')` (lines 119-145) produces `'test.txt'`, which does **not** start with `normalizedRoot + '/'` nor equal `normalizedRoot` (check at lines 182-185), so the function falls through and returns `true` at line 189; consequently, `requiresWorkspaceBoundaryConfirmation()` returns `needsConfirmation: true` at lines 217-222, incorrectly treating an in-workspace relative file like `test.txt` as outside the workspace. ```
[Fix in Cursor](https://app.codeant.ai/fix-in-ide?tool=cursor&prompt=This%20is%20a%20comment%20left%20during%20a%20code%20review.%0A%0A%2A%2APath%3A%2A%2A%20packages%2Fshared%2Flib%2Fworkspace-boundary.ts%0A%2A%2ALine%3A%2A%2A%20167%3A186%0A%2A%2AComment%3A%2A%2A%0A%09%2ALogic%20Error%3A%20The%20boundary%20check%20compares%20raw%20normalized%20strings%20and%20never%20resolves%20relative%20targets%20against%20the%20workspace%20root%2C%20even%20though%20the%20function%20accepts%20relative%20paths.%20A%20relative%20path%20like%20%60src%2Ffile.ts%60%20will%20not%20start%20with%20an%20absolute%20workspace%20root%2C%20so%20it%20is%20incorrectly%20marked%20as%20outside.%20Resolve%20relative%20targets%20against%20the%20workspace%20root%20%28or%20cwd%29%20before%20comparison.%0A%0AValidate%20the%20correctness%20of%20the%20flagged%20issue.%20If%20correct%2C%20How%20can%20I%20resolve%20this%3F%20If%20you%20propose%20a%20fix%2C%20implement%20it%20and%20please%20make%20it%20concise.%0AOnce%20fix%20is%20implemented%2C%20also%20check%20other%20comments%20on%20the%20same%20PR%2C%20and%20ask%20user%20if%20the%20user%20wants%20to%20fix%20the%20rest%20of%20the%20comments%20as%20well.%20if%20said%20yes%2C%20then%20fetch%20all%20the%20comments%20validate%20the%20correctness%20and%20implement%20a%20minimal%20fix%0A) | [Fix in VSCode Claude](https://app.codeant.ai/fix-in-ide?tool=vscode-claude&prompt=This%20is%20a%20comment%20left%20during%20a%20code%20review.%0A%0A%2A%2APath%3A%2A%2A%20packages%2Fshared%2Flib%2Fworkspace-boundary.ts%0A%2A%2ALine%3A%2A%2A%20167%3A186%0A%2A%2AComment%3A%2A%2A%0A%09%2ALogic%20Error%3A%20The%20boundary%20check%20compares%20raw%20normalized%20strings%20and%20never%20resolves%20relative%20targets%20against%20the%20workspace%20root%2C%20even%20though%20the%20function%20accepts%20relative%20paths.%20A%20relative%20path%20like%20%60src%2Ffile.ts%60%20will%20not%20start%20with%20an%20absolute%20workspace%20root%2C%20so%20it%20is%20incorrectly%20marked%20as%20outside.%20Resolve%20relative%20targets%20against%20the%20workspace%20root%20%28or%20cwd%29%20before%20comparison.%0A%0AValidate%20the%20correctness%20of%20the%20flagged%20issue.%20If%20correct%2C%20How%20can%20I%20resolve%20this%3F%20If%20you%20propose%20a%20fix%2C%20implement%20it%20and%20please%20make%20it%20concise.%0AOnce%20fix%20is%20implemented%2C%20also%20check%20other%20comments%20on%20the%20same%20PR%2C%20and%20ask%20user%20if%20the%20user%20wants%20to%20fix%20the%20rest%20of%20the%20comments%20as%20well.%20if%20said%20yes%2C%20then%20fetch%20all%20the%20comments%20validate%20the%20correctness%20and%20implement%20a%20minimal%20fix%0A) *(Use Cmd/Ctrl + Click for best experience)*
Prompt for AI Agent 🤖 ```mdx This is a comment left during a code review. **Path:** packages/shared/lib/workspace-boundary.ts **Line:** 167:186 **Comment:** *Logic Error: The boundary check compares raw normalized strings and never resolves relative targets against the workspace root, even though the function accepts relative paths. A relative path like `src/file.ts` will not start with an absolute workspace root, so it is incorrectly marked as outside. Resolve relative targets against the workspace root (or cwd) before comparison. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. Once fix is implemented, also check other comments on the same PR, and ask user if the user wants to fix the rest of the comments as well. if said yes, then fetch all the comments validate the correctness and implement a minimal fix ```

**Suggestion:** The `forceFlag` branch still returns `shouldConfirm: true`, which causes callers to treat force mode as requiring confirmation instead of bypassing it. This contradicts the function contract and can block `--force` workflows. Return a non-confirming result (or `null`) when force mode is active. [logic error]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ⚠️ Force flag users still see workspace boundary confirmations. - ⚠️ Automation scripts using --force may hang awaiting prompts. - ⚠️ Semantics contradict documentation, confusing CLI or UI consumers. ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Inspect `buildWorkspaceBoundaryWarning()` in `packages/shared/lib/workspace-boundary.ts:243-274`, whose docstring at lines 236-242 states it "Returns null if no confirmation is needed (safe operation or force flag set)" and which is re-exported for consumers at `packages/shared/cli/lib/workspace-boundary-cli.ts:11-22` and `web/lib/agent-bins/workspace-boundary.ts:19-29`. 2. Consider a caller pattern typical for a confirmation dialog: `const warning = buildWorkspaceBoundaryWarning({...}); if (!warning) proceed(); else if (warning.shouldConfirm) showPrompt();`, using `forceFlag: true` to represent a `--force` mode that should bypass prompts. 3. With `WORKSPACE_ROOT` pointing to a workspace directory (per `resolveWorkspaceRoot()` at lines 74-88), call `buildWorkspaceBoundaryWarning({ operation: 'delete', targetPath: '/etc/passwd', forceFlag: true })`; `requiresWorkspaceBoundaryConfirmation()` (lines 201-223) will correctly compute `needsConfirmation: true` because `/etc/passwd` is outside the workspace. 4. Instead of returning `null` when `forceFlag` is set, `buildWorkspaceBoundaryWarning()` executes the `if (forceFlag)` branch at lines 255-262 and returns an object with `shouldConfirm: true`, so any consumer using `warning.shouldConfirm` to gate prompts will still show a confirmation even in force mode, contradicting the documented "force flag bypass" semantics and the intent expressed in the integration test `should handle force flag bypass` in `packages/shared/cli/__tests__/workspace-boundary-integration.test.ts:361-372` (which assumes force mode bypasses prompts while still recording the check). ```
[Fix in Cursor](https://app.codeant.ai/fix-in-ide?tool=cursor&prompt=This%20is%20a%20comment%20left%20during%20a%20code%20review.%0A%0A%2A%2APath%3A%2A%2A%20packages%2Fshared%2Flib%2Fworkspace-boundary.ts%0A%2A%2ALine%3A%2A%2A%20255%3A263%0A%2A%2AComment%3A%2A%2A%0A%09%2ALogic%20Error%3A%20The%20%60forceFlag%60%20branch%20still%20returns%20%60shouldConfirm%3A%20true%60%2C%20which%20causes%20callers%20to%20treat%20force%20mode%20as%20requiring%20confirmation%20instead%20of%20bypassing%20it.%20This%20contradicts%20the%20function%20contract%20and%20can%20block%20%60--force%60%20workflows.%20Return%20a%20non-confirming%20result%20%28or%20%60null%60%29%20when%20force%20mode%20is%20active.%0A%0AValidate%20the%20correctness%20of%20the%20flagged%20issue.%20If%20correct%2C%20How%20can%20I%20resolve%20this%3F%20If%20you%20propose%20a%20fix%2C%20implement%20it%20and%20please%20make%20it%20concise.%0AOnce%20fix%20is%20implemented%2C%20also%20check%20other%20comments%20on%20the%20same%20PR%2C%20and%20ask%20user%20if%20the%20user%20wants%20to%20fix%20the%20rest%20of%20the%20comments%20as%20well.%20if%20said%20yes%2C%20then%20fetch%20all%20the%20comments%20validate%20the%20correctness%20and%20implement%20a%20minimal%20fix%0A) | [Fix in VSCode Claude](https://app.codeant.ai/fix-in-ide?tool=vscode-claude&prompt=This%20is%20a%20comment%20left%20during%20a%20code%20review.%0A%0A%2A%2APath%3A%2A%2A%20packages%2Fshared%2Flib%2Fworkspace-boundary.ts%0A%2A%2ALine%3A%2A%2A%20255%3A263%0A%2A%2AComment%3A%2A%2A%0A%09%2ALogic%20Error%3A%20The%20%60forceFlag%60%20branch%20still%20returns%20%60shouldConfirm%3A%20true%60%2C%20which%20causes%20callers%20to%20treat%20force%20mode%20as%20requiring%20confirmation%20instead%20of%20bypassing%20it.%20This%20contradicts%20the%20function%20contract%20and%20can%20block%20%60--force%60%20workflows.%20Return%20a%20non-confirming%20result%20%28or%20%60null%60%29%20when%20force%20mode%20is%20active.%0A%0AValidate%20the%20correctness%20of%20the%20flagged%20issue.%20If%20correct%2C%20How%20can%20I%20resolve%20this%3F%20If%20you%20propose%20a%20fix%2C%20implement%20it%20and%20please%20make%20it%20concise.%0AOnce%20fix%20is%20implemented%2C%20also%20check%20other%20comments%20on%20the%20same%20PR%2C%20and%20ask%20user%20if%20the%20user%20wants%20to%20fix%20the%20rest%20of%20the%20comments%20as%20well.%20if%20said%20yes%2C%20then%20fetch%20all%20the%20comments%20validate%20the%20correctness%20and%20implement%20a%20minimal%20fix%0A) This contradicts the function contract and can block `--force` workflows. Return a non-confirming result (or `null`) when force mode is active. Validate the correctness of the flagged issue. If correct, How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, implement it and please make it concise. Once fix is implemented, also check other comments on the same PR, and ask user if the user wants to fix the rest of the comments as well. if said yes, then fetch all the comments validate the correctness and implement a minimal fix ```

**Suggestion:** The "legacy numeric ID" branch is a no-op: it parses a number and immediately returns the original ID string without attempting the fallback user lookup. This breaks email-strategy resolution for migrated users whose old IDs need lookup before sending `user_id` to Arcade. Mirror the existing fallback pattern used in the other Arcade auth routes by actually querying for that legacy ID. [logic error]
Severity Level: Major ⚠️ ```mdx - ❌ Arcade custom verifier sends numeric IDs instead of emails. - ⚠️ Migrated users with legacy IDs may not match Arcade. - ⚠️ Behavior inconsistent with `/api/integrations/arcade/auth` and `/token`. ```
Steps of Reproduction ✅ ```mdx 1. Enable the Arcade custom verifier by setting `ARCADE_CUSTOM_VERIFIER_ENABLED=true` and `ARCADE_USER_ID_STRATEGY=email` as documented in `env.example:2050-2052`, then start the Next.js app so `GET /api/auth/arcade/custom-verifier` is active (`web/app/api/auth/arcade/custom-verifier/route.ts:85-136`). 2. Use a migrated installation scenario where `resolveRequestAuth` (`web/app/api/auth/arcade/custom-verifier/route.ts:99-103`) returns an authenticated user whose `userId` is a legacy numeric string (e.g., `"123"`). The existence of this migration case is documented in the other Arcade routes, e.g. `web/app/api/integrations/arcade/token/route.ts:16-23` and `web/app/api/integrations/arcade/auth/route.ts:19-25` where comments state "handles migration cases where old numeric IDs might be referenced." 3. When `GET /api/auth/arcade/custom-verifier` is called with a valid `flow_id` and token, the handler at `web/app/api/auth/arcade/custom-verifier/route.ts:111-113` invokes `resolveArcadeUserId(authResult.userId)`. For a numeric `appUserId`, `resolveArcadeUserId` (`route.ts:14-29`) first calls `authService.getUserById(appUserId)` with the string `"123"` (line 19). If this does not return a user with `email` (e.g., because the primary ID is now a UUID), execution reaches the "legacy numeric ID" branch at lines 23-26. 4. In that branch (`web/app/api/auth/arcade/custom-verifier/route.ts:23-26`), the code parses `const numeric = Number(appUserId);` and, if it's not `NaN`, immediately executes `return appUserId;` without performing any additional lookup. This is a no-op compared to the default `return appUserId;` at line 29 and diverges from the intended legacy fallback implemented in the other Arcade routes (`web/app/api/integrations/arcade/auth/route.ts:19-25` and `web/app/api/integrations/arcade/token/route.ts:16-23`), where the numeric value is used to call `authService.getUserById(String(numeric))` and potentially return `user.email`. As a result, for legacy numeric IDs in the custom verifier flow, `confirmUserWithArcade` (`route.ts:32-63`) is called with `user_id` set to the numeric ID string instead of the resolved email, breaking the "email" strategy resolution specifically for this custom verifier endpoint. ```
[Fix in
